assumptions: The question concerns consolidated financial statements under IFRS.
The intragroup dividend gives rise to a recognised intragroup monetary receivable/payable once declared and recorded.
The issue is limited to accounting models relevant to foreign currency exposure and hedge accounting for that recognised position.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 39 and IFRS 9 identify fair value hedge as a top-level hedge accounting model for recognised assets or liabilities exposed to changes in fair value attributable to a particular risk.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 39 and IFRS 9 identify cash flow hedge as a separate top-level hedge accounting model for exposures to variability in cash flows attributable to a particular risk.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 39, IFRS 9, IAS 21 and IFRIC 16 identify hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation as a distinct hedge accounting model for foreign currency exposures.

approaches.label: Ordinary foreign currency accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 21 provides the non-hedge-accounting model for recognised foreign currency monetary items, under which exchange differences are generally recognised in profit or loss unless the item forms part of a net investment.
